title: Pasqal and Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp. II File Form F-4 SEC Registration for $2 Billion Public Nasdaq Merger - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Pasqal filed its Form F-4 for a Nasdaq SPAC merger with Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp. II at a $2.0B pre-money valuation and ~$500M gross proceeds, backing a neutral-atom hardware roadmap targeting 10,000+ physical and 200+ logical qubits per QPU by end-2029.

insights.claim: Pasqal's Nasdaq merger with Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp. II (Nasdaq: BBCQ) values the French quantum developer at a $2.0 billion pre-money equity valuation and is structured to deliver ~$500 million in gross proceeds assuming no redemptions and closing of concurrent convertible financing.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Sets a valuation benchmark ($2.0B pre-money) for pure-play neutral-atom quantum; redemption risk is the key variable on actual cash raised.
insights.quote: a pre-money equity valuation of $2.0 billion USD and is structured to deliver approximately $500 million USD in gross proceeds to the combined entity, assuming no shareholder redemptions

insights.claim: Post-close the combined company will operate as Pasqal Holding SA and list ordinary shares on Nasdaq.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Identifies the go-forward listed entity name for tracking the ticker post-merger.
insights.locator: Upon regulatory enforcement and formal shareholder clearance

insights.claim: Pasqal has seven operational neutral-atom QPUs deployed in the field and three additional systems in active production, operating two manufacturing installations across France and Canada with end-to-end supply-chain sovereignty.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Claimed as one of the largest physical footprints among pure-play quantum firms — a tangible moat versus cloud-only competitors.
insights.quote: one of the largest physical infrastructure footprints among pure-play quantum computing firms globally, with seven active quantum processing units (QPUs) deployed in the field

insights.claim: Pasqal's roadmap targets scaling neutral-atom processors to over 10,000 physical qubits and 200 logical qubits per QPU by the end of 2029.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A concrete, dated scaling target (end-2029) to hold management accountable against; slippage would signal execution risk.
insights.quote: scaling its neutral-atom processors to control over 10,000 physical qubits and 200 logical qubits per QPU by the end of 2029

insights.claim: Pasqal's hardware architecture executes both analog quantum simulation and digital gate-based fault-tolerant computing on the same physical platform, backed by a 40+ client/partner ecosystem including Aramco, CMA CGM, OVHcloud, and Thales.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Dual analog+digital capability on one platform broadens addressable use cases versus single-mode competitors.
insights.locator: The company’s hardware architecture is designed

insights.claim: Pasqal was co-founded by 2022 Nobel Laureate Alain Aspect and John Stewart Bell Prize winner Antoine Browaeys.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Nobel-tier founding pedigree is a credibility/marketing asset for the public raise.

insights.claim: Pasqal's platform natively supports NVIDIA CUDA-Q via its Quantum Resource Management Interface runtime, letting neutral-atom systems be orchestrated through standard Slurm-based HPC workflows, and is being deployed at CINECA to accelerate the Leonardo pre-exascale supercomputer.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: NVIDIA CUDA-Q compatibility plus a live supercomputer deployment lowers integration friction and validates the hybrid HPC-quantum go-to-market.
insights.quote: native compatibility with the NVIDIA CUDA-Q framework via its Quantum Resource Management Interface (QRMI) runtime
insights.locator: The capital injection from the public listing

insights.claim: Pasqal validated the industry's first solution of differential equations using two logical qubits on a live neutral-atom processor, showing logical-level erasure mapping cuts physical gate noise for a measurable precision advantage over bare physical qubits.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Concrete logical-qubit advantage demonstration supports the fault-tolerance narrative underpinning the valuation.
insights.quote: the industry’s first solution of differential equations using two logical qubits on an active neutral-atom processor
